We are going to take the metro to the city center.
I am going to ask at the front desk for a map.
On Thursday, Rosangela is going to take the tram to Belem.
Are you going to come back today?
On Tuesday, we are going to see the tower in Belem.
On Wednesday, we are going to transfer at Rossio.
Excuse me. Could you tell me how to get to Belem, please?
Is this the right platform for the airport?
How long does it take to get to the city center?
I would rather take the metro than a taxi. It is faster.
Good morning. Could you tell me how to get to Belem, please? My husband and I are going to visit the tower today.
Good morning. Belem, of course. You are not going to take the metro: you are going to take the tram. Number fifteen, from Praca da Figueira.
The tram, not the metro. Thank you. And where do I buy the ticket? Is there a ticket machine here?
Yes, behind you, on your left. The fare is one euro and eighty. Are you going to come back today?
Yes, we are. We are going to come back in the afternoon, after lunch.
Then buy a round trip, not a one-way ticket. Same price, and you are not going to stand in this line again.
I am sorry, I did not catch that. Could you say it more slowly, please?
Of course. Buy a round trip, one ticket, to go and to come back. Then walk to platform two. And do not transfer: tram fifteen goes all the way to Belem.
Now I understand. Thank you very much. And how long does it take to get to Belem?
About twenty-five minutes. And if you are tired tonight, take a ride-share back to the hotel: after eleven the metro is closed. Enjoy the tower!
Two round trips, please. We are going to come back this afternoon.
I would rather take the metro than a taxi.

Good morning, and welcome to the Lisbon metro. Please listen to this information before you travel. First, your ticket. The ticket machines are in front of you, next to the entrance. You can choose English on the screen: press the small flag, on the top right. On the machine you can buy a one-way ticket, for one trip only, or a round trip, to go and to come back on the same day. The fare for one trip is one euro and eighty. A round trip is three euros and sixty. It is the same price, but you only stand in the line one time. Second, the platform. After the ticket machine, look at the big sign above your head. The sign does not give you the name of your station: it gives you the name of the last station on the line. So look for the last station, and walk to that platform. Third, the transfer. Some trips are direct, and some are not. If you need to transfer, you change from one line to another inside the station, and you do not pay again: your ticket is good for the whole trip. The station for a transfer is Baixa-Chiado, in the center. And last, the tram and the bus. Your metro ticket does not work on the tram. For the tram and for the bus, you buy a different ticket, at the bus stop or from the driver. Thank you very much, and have a good day in Lisbon.

Hello. My name is Beatriz, and I am waiting for the tram, like you. Is this your first day in Lisbon? Oh, then let me tell you what I am going to do this week. You can copy me! Tomorrow I am going to take tram twenty-eight, in the morning, early. Why early? Because at ten o'clock it is full of people, and you are going to travel standing up, with your nose in somebody's back. Not nice. Tram twenty-eight goes up the hill to Alfama, and it is the most beautiful trip in this city. On Wednesday I am going to take the train to Sintra, and I am going to see the palace. And on Thursday? Nothing. On Thursday I am going to sit in a café and do absolutely nothing. That is my favorite plan of the week. Now, one small piece of advice. I would rather take the metro than a taxi, always. The traffic here is terrible, and the metro is fast and cheap. But at night, after eleven, the metro is closed, so I would rather take a ride-share. And my husband? Oh, he does not like the tram. He says it is too slow, so he is going to walk everywhere. He is going to arrive tired, and I am going to arrive happy. Well, that is my tram. Enjoy the city!
